channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: The iPhone 17 Air could be first in line for this cutting-edge display technology
item.description: The iPhone 17 rumors are certainly up and running now – and the latest to reach us is around displays.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: AI-powered transcription leaks workplace secrets
item.description: Alex Bilzerian shared on X that the Otter AI platform shared confidential information, The Washington Post first reported.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, NewsNation

item.title: Why the OpenAI-to-Anthropic pipeline remains so strong
item.description: The pipeline from OpenAI to Anthropic remains strong as ever, with cofounder Durk Kingma becoming the latest high-profile figure to make the move. On Tuesday, Kingma, one of the lesser-known cofounders of OpenAI, joined Anthropic, best known for developing the rival AI chatbot Claude.
item.dc:creator: Janya Sundar, Fast Company

item.title: The shocking rise and fall of elite cybersecurity firm IronNet
item.description: The future was once dazzling for IronNet. Founded by a former director of the National Security Agency and stacked with elite members of the U.S. intelligence establishment, IronNet promised it was going to revolutionize the way governments and corporations combat cyberattacks.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: What to stream: Coldplay, an unhinged Charlie Puth, 'Salem’s Lot,' Sarah Paulson and NHL 25
item.description: Coldplay's 10th studio album and the American Music Awards celebrating their 50th anniversary are some of the new television, films, music and games headed to a device near you.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Google’s ad-tech antitrust trial: Wrapping up the case for the defense
item.description: And now we wait. Last week, Google did its best to refute the US Department of Justice’s case that the company has built a series of monopolies in the markets for ad serving, ad exchanges, and ad networks, and the DOJ made its rebuttal. Closing arguments will be heard Nov.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Civilization 7 makers work with Shawnee to bring sincere representation of the tribe to the game
item.description: MIAMI, Okla. (AP) — Shawnee Tribe Chief Ben Barnes grew up playing video games, including “probably hundreds of hours” colonizing a distant planet in the 1999 title Sid Meier's Alpha Centauri.
item.dc:creator: GRAHAM LEE BREWER and MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: How many jobs are available in technology in the US?
item.description: Unemployment data released today showed surprising growth overall for technology job listings and hiring, but also marked shift in the kind of workers organizations need — with AI talent no longer at the top.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Scammers are flooding Trump’s new crypto exchange
item.description: Back in September, Donald Trump announced his new crypto exchange, World Liberty Financial (WLFI). Now, registered investors can officially sign up for the whitelist—assuming they can avoid the scammers.
item.dc:creator: Henry Chandonnet, Fast Company

item.title: The EU wants to know more about social media algorithms
item.description: Via the Digital Services Regulation (DSA), the European Commission has requested information from Youtube, Snapchat and Tiktok about which parameters their algorithms use to recommend social media content to users.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: The US and Microsoft disrupt a Russian hacking group targeting American officials and nonprofits
item.description: U.S. authorities and Microsoft say hackers linked to Russian intelligence targeted dozens of former military and intelligence officials, journalists and civil society groups with a sophisticated campaign designed to steal information.
item.dc:creator: David Klepper, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Google CEO eyeing electricity from nuclear plants for its data centers
item.description: Google CEO Sundar Pichai is considering using electricity from nuclear plants to power his company’s data centers, as the tech giant contends with the expansive energy demands of artificial intelligence (AI).
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: OpenAI demands investors shun rivals such as Anthropic, Elon Musk’s xAI
item.description: OpenAI has raised $6.6 billion from investors like Thrive Capital and Tiger Global, but the AI company also sought assurances that investors would avoid funding five competing firms, according to a Reuters report.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Tres cosas que hay que hacer bien en la gestión de datos para proyectos de IA generativa
item.description: Según Kari Briski, vicepresidente de Modelos, Software y Servicios de IA en Nvidia, implementar con éxito la inteligencia artificial (IA) generativa depende de la gestión eficaz de los datos y de evaluar cómo los diferentes modelos trabajan juntos para servir a un caso de uso específico.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Let your iPhone read aloud what you can't see
item.description: The new Reader Mode in iOS 18’s Magnifier app is here to help iPhone users read something small or in poor lighting. All you have to do is snap a picture.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hurricane Helene brings climate change to forefront of the presidential campaign
item.description: The devastation wrought by Hurricane Helene has brought climate change to the forefront of the presidential campaign.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Daly, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Cyber Leaders Exchange 2024: CISA’s Jeff Greene on challenging the status quo
item.description: The new executive assistant director for cybersecurity at CISA details how sharing threat intelligence more broadly pays off. The post Cyber Leaders Exchange 2024: CISA’s Jeff Greene on challenging the status quo first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: Details from New Mexico's lawsuit against Snap show site failed to act on reports of sextortion
item.description: Snapchat failed to act on “rampant” reports of child grooming, sextortion and other dangers to minors on its platform, according to a newly unredacted complaint against the company filed by New Mexico's attorney general. Attorney General Raúl Torrez filed the original complaint on Sept.
item.dc:creator: BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, KRON4
